impressum.subtitle: Disclosure pursuant to § 25 of the Austrian Media Act
impressum.copyright-text: The content of this website is subject to various intellectual property rights (e.g., copyright) to the extent legally permissible. Any use or distribution of provided material that is prohibited by copyright law requires the written consent of the website operator.
impressum.disclaimer-text-1: Despite careful review of the content, the operator of this website assumes no liability for the content of external links. The operators of the linked websites are solely responsible for their content. Should you nevertheless become aware of any outgoing links that lead to a website with illegal activity/information, please inform us so that we can remove them immediately.
impressum.disclaimer-text-2: The operator of this website takes the utmost care to respect the copyrights of third parties. Should you nevertheless become aware of a copyright infringement, please inform us accordingly. Upon notification of such infringements, we will remove the affected content immediately.

home.paragraph1: Hi! I'm Adrian, {{ age }} years old and I'm highly interested in Computer Science with a strong passion for backend development and a growing interest in frontend technologies. Over the past years, I’ve developed my skills in various programming languages, including Kotlin, C# and Java. I am working at RZL Software after finishing my Masters Degree in April 2025. In my free time I love to work on side-projects like this website.
home.paragraph2: This website is a space where I aim to showcase projects, share my journey, and connect with others.
home.paragraph3: Besides of coding, I’m eager to stay active as a firefighter in my home village and love being active through volleyball and cycling.

timeline-texts.rzl: After my journey in Linz, studying computer science I returned to my hometown, and I am more than happy to have joined RZL again as a Software Developer. Here I am working with skilled people, implementing new features for the -next generation of the RZL Board in .NET and WPF.
timeline-texts.fabasoft: In February 2025 I started my first real job in the role as Software Developer working full-time at Fabasoft. I was assigned to the team Relations and worked on the internal tool sharing the same name. At this company I learned their homemade programming language app.ducx which is used to develop their cloud service.
timeline-texts.dynatrace-intern: My second (and last) internship was at Dynatrace. Dynatrace is a big international company with roots in Linz, which is also the location where I took my internship. I got placed in the Cloud Access Engineering team and got tasked with creating a prototype for improving their process of reviewing permissions in order to stay SOX compliant. My major takeaways from working at this company are a kind team, the agile scrum workflow at work, and improvements in my Spring and Kotlin skills.
timeline-texts.rzl-intern: My first internship was at RZL. This is a medium-sized company in the heart of the Innviertel. At RZL they tasked me to help working on an internal tool which distributes their software across their servers. The people there are very kind and my mentor was always there if I had any question. The main thing I took from this internship is, how to work with git and a better knowledge about C#.
timeline-texts.ssw: As a student I quickly decided to take a tutoring job. Since I liked the lectures from SSW best, I took the first opportunity to take a position at their institute. At this position my task changed over the years. First it was to answer Students' questions in the Tutorial for Software Development 1, and correct homeworks in the lectures Software Development 2 and later the follow up courses Practical in Software Development 2 and Compiler Construction.
timeline-texts.naschmax: At Naschmax I got into first contact with working at age 17. I worked here about once a week on the weekend while going to school. While being employed, my task was to sell sweets in various locations in Austria. Mainly in the upper austrian region, but sometimes we also went to bigger venues like the Dult in Salzburg or the Kirchtag in Villach. A fun event was the Linzer Urfix which took place twice a year.
timeline-texts.master: For my masters degree I specialized in the field of Software Engineering. My masters thesis is about creating personalized semester schedules for students at JKU. I have written the tool in collaboration with the Department of Information Management and the Institute for System Software. The thesis can be found here.
timeline-texts.bachelor: Right after school I started studying Mathematics at JKU, but quickly I realized that this topic is too dry. Immediately in the next semester I switched to studying Informatics. Life has been better since.
timeline-texts.borg: In Austria one has to attain Matura before being admitted to University. During the time in school I got elected as a vice student representative and in our term we achieved that our school adapts some nice merch. The entire planning, and execution was done by us (kinda proud of that one). The end of this time was awarded with an diploma with excellence.

papers.master: My Master Thesis covers a project in which I create an application for students. This application allows them to plan their schedule for the upcoming semester at JKU.
papers.seminar2: In the course of our master studies Alexander Voglsperger and I described some ideas on Data Structure Analysis and Memory Bloat in Unmanaged Languages.
papers.bachelor: As my Bachelor Project I have created a Moodle manager, which downloads Homeworks from Moodle and distributes them via Discord.
papers.seminar1: In this paper we describe an application with a few imaginary Banks and Stores, so that we can try out and compare a few solutions by working with the async/await feature in C#.
papers.course: In this paper Alexander Voglsperger and I compared a few Live Sharing tools. This work is a paper to train the process of writing, in order to write good bachelor- and master thesis later on in our university careers.
papers.vwa-description: In order to get admitted to university in austria up until 2024 it was required to write some kind of pre-scientific work. My work written in german and talks about the number pi.
